lede: A look at August 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Louis XVI and Michel Rocard, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Natascha Kampusch
events.desc: Natascha Kampusch, who had been abducted at the age of ten, escapes from her captor Wolfgang Přiklopil, after eight years of captivity.

events.title: World Wide Web
events.desc: The World Wide Web is opened to the public.

events.title: Armenia
events.desc: Armenia declares its independence from the Soviet Union.

events.title: Singing Revolution
events.desc: Singing Revolution: Two million people from Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania stand on the Vilnius–Tallinn road, holding hands.

events.title: Cruise of the Kings
events.desc: The Cruise of the Kings, a royal cruise organised by the Queen Consort of Greece, Frederica of Hanover, departs from Marseille, France.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union sign the Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact. In a secret protocol to the pact, Poland, Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, and Romania are divided into German and Soviet "spheres of influence".

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The British Expeditionary Force and the French Fifth Army begin their Great Retreat before the German Army.

events.title: Southern Cross Expedition
events.desc: The Southern Cross Expedition, the first British venture of the Heroic Age of Antarctic Exploration, departs from London.

events.title: Albert Bridge, London
events.desc: The Albert Bridge in Chelsea, London opens.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte leaves Egypt for France en route to seizing power.

births.name: Michel Rocard
births.line: French civil servant and politician, 160th Prime Minister of France

births.name: Peter Lilley
births.line: English politician, Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills

deaths.text: Adolf Loos, Austrian architect and theoretician, designed Villa Müller
William Wallace, Scottish knight and rebel leader (born c.1270)

observances.text: Christian feast day: Asterius, Claudius, and Neon
Christian feast day: Quiriacus and companions, of Ostia
